entry.title: Financial Accounting for Hierarchical Setup in Item-Based Services
entry.content:
1 Overview

Dear Colleagues, 

I would like to present a new hierarchical setup in item-based services. We will explore the financial integration of a service contract and service orders with advanced execution. This scenario applies only to on-premise / private cloud environments. The integration with classical item-based service items will be available in future releases. The screenshots below are taken from the OP2023 FP2 system. 

I begin with a business example and provide a functional overview, followed by an end-to-end process demonstration of the system. I will highlight the differences between the service contract root item (new in OP2023 FP2) and non-root items (attribution only) in the service contract/order financial hierarchy. Finally, I will conclude with deeper insights into process control and system configuration.

This blog is the follow-up blog to the next two blogs:

  • New Financial Accounting for Service Management in S/4HANA Cloud by Stefan Walz (https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blogs-by-sap/new-financial-accounting-for-service-management-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13489567)
  • Financial Accounting 4 Advanced Execution Service Scenario in SAP S/4HANA By Stefan Walz and Gabi Hoffmann (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/financial-accounting-4-advanced-execution-service-scenario-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13858752)

I highly recommend reviewing these blogs first. Also, you can take a look at the SAP help page (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c9b5e9de6e674fb99fff88d72c352291/cd63e0856e1d458499e5a99de6b0378b.html) 

 

 

2 Business Scenario Overview

Business Example: SmartFix Maintenance Services

Industry: HVAC, home appliances, or industrial equipment maintenance

Business Model Overview:

SmartFix provides preventive and on-demand maintenance services for residential and commercial clients. Customers pay a monthly or annual subscription fee, while actual service costs are incurred only when maintenance or repair is performed.

 

Revenue Model (Subscription-Based)

Customers subscribe to a tiered maintenance plan:

  1. Basic Plan ($1200/month per device) – Covers monthly/quaterly inspections and minor fixes.
  2. Standard Plan ($1500/month per device) – Includes preventive maintenance and priority scheduling.
  3. Premium Plan ($3000/month per device) – Covers full maintenance, labor, and minor parts replacement.

The revenue is managed by the SAP S4HANA Service Contract.

 

Cost Structure (Service Orders-Based)

SmartFix incurs costs only when maintenance or repair work is actually performed, such as:

  • Technician labor costs (hourly wage + travel costs).
  • Replacement parts/materials (if not included in the plan).
  • Operational expenses (vehicle costs, tools, etc.).

Each service request triggers a service order, which is an operational cost to the business.

 

Example Scenario:

  1. A customer subscribes for 3 months (2 months and 30 days) to the Basic Plan for 1 HVAC units → Pays $1200/month -> totalling $3560 of total income ($1200 + $1200 + $1160). The Basic Plan has a fixed scope of work with total planned costs of $2834,00
  2. After 1 month, one unit requires a repair, generating a service order. SmartFix incurs costs for the technician visit, which includes:
    • Labor: $1660,08
    • Parts: $40,5
    • Total Cost: $1700,58
  1. After 2 months, another unit requires a repair, generating the second service order. SmartFix incurs costs for the technician visit, which includes:
    • Labor: $1106,72
    • Parts: $27
    • Total Cost: $1133,72
  1. Over 3 months, SmartFix received $3560 in subscription revenue but only incurred $2834 in costs, maintaining profitability.

 

Why Does This Model Work?

  • Predictable cash flow: Subscription revenue ensures consistent income.
  • Optimized service costs: Only incurred when necessary, reducing unnecessary expenses.
  • Scalability: More subscribers increase revenue, while efficient service management keeps costs controlled.
  • Customer stickiness: Subscriptions encourage long-term customer relationships.

 

 

3 Functional Overview

First, let’s understand the difference between a hierarchical setup with root and non-root service contract items.

3.1 Service Contract Item as an attribution parameter only (non-root item)

In the basic hierarchical item-based services setup the service contract item is not marked as a "root" item. The service contract item serves only as an attribution parameter. Each service order and service contract item has independent billing and revenue recognition postings.

Figure 1. Financial Process Flow - Service Contract is an Attribution Parameter Only (Non-Root)
 
You can find the revenue recognition rules for this setup below.
  • Service Contracts: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f63dd39a28bb4b90adbf9e608aff58ea/d372d2199d084b3f9d2...
  • Service Orders with Advance Execution: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f63dd39a28bb4b90adbf9e608aff58ea/3034581971d94efa831...

In the current blog, I will not focus on this scenario. The non-root scenario was introduced some time ago and well explained in the blog of Stefan Walz (https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blogs-by-sap/new-financial-accounting-for-service-management-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13489567).

 

3.2 Service Contract Item as a Root Item in Finance Hierarchy

This is a scenario where a service contract item is created as a root item. You can see that there is no revenue recognition on the service order level.

Figure 2. Financial Process Flow - Service Contract as a Root Object in the Hierarchy

Below, you can see how the postings are recorded in the Universal Journal (ACDOCA).

Figure 3. Hierarchical Setup: Service Contract Item as Root Object – Financial Transactional Data Overview

The service orders have no revenue recognition key assigned.

Figure 1. Hierarchical Setup: Service Contract Item as Root Object – Financial View of Service DocumentFigure 4. Hierarchical Setup: Service Contract Item as Root Object – Financial View of Service Document

In the example above, no revenues are posted to the service order because its billing relevance is set to “N – Not Relevant for Billing.” In OP2023 FP3, billing relevance "billing on completion" for the service order is allowed.

Please also have a look at the field "Hierarchy Processing Indicator". It is filled with the value "01" for all service contract and order items if the service contract item category is marked as a "root item". 

 

3.3 Item-Based Services Hierarchical Setup: Difference between “root” and “non-root” service contract items

Please see the hierarchy integration in the item-based services comparison matrix for “root” and “non-root” below.

Table 1. Non-Root & Root Service Contract Item Hierarchical Setup Comparison Table

Where

  • OR - Order
  • SO - Service Order
  • SC – Service Contract
  • AA – Accounting Assignment

 

4 System Walkthrough

Now, I will present an end-to-end process for basic attribution (non-root) and root service contract items, using two different revenue recognition approaches.

4.1 Service Contract & Service Order Creation

4.1.1 Financials View for Service Documents

At first, we create a service contract, item 100 is a non-root item (item category is “SCN”) and item 200 is a root item in the finance hierarchy (item category is “YCNH”).

Figure 5. Service Contract Creation

In addition, several service orders are created and assigned to service contract items according to the financial process flow diagrams in Figures 1 and 2.

Figure 6. Financial View of Service Document

You can see that all service objects assigned to the basic (non-root) service contract item 100 have a revenue recognition key (Column “RA Key”, field “REV_REC_KEY”) assigned.

In the setup with a root service contract item (HIER_PROCG_IND = "01"), only the service contract has a revenue recognition key. All service orders are not relevant for billing (SERVICE_DOC_BILLING_RELEVANCE = "N") and revenue recognition (REV_REC_KEY is empty).

 

4.1.2 Plan Data Update

4.1.2.1 Plan Revenues for Service Contracts

Planned revenue is calculated as the sum of all billing plan amounts for the service contract item. For example, item 200 has three billing plan items.

Figure 7. Service Contract Billing Plan

If we sum up all three billing plan items, we receive 3.865,67 EUR in total.

Now, let’s look at the plan data table (ACDOCP). The output is filtered by the relevant service objects and ongoing plan data categories. In our case, these are "PSERV02" and "PMORDER02". For more details, please refer to the customizing section.

Figure 8. Plan Data Overview for Service Contracts

*The plan revenues are updated for both items (root and non-root ).

Note: You can see the same data in standard Fiori Apps like “Service – Plan/Actuals” or “Financial Plan Data Analysis”.

Figure 9. Service - Plan/Actuals Report
 

4.1.2.2 Plan Data Update for Service Orders

The difference between root and non-root setup in the plan data update for service orders is that in setup with “root” flagged service contract item service orders are not relevant for billing (FPS2). It means no plan revenues to be updated for such orders.

Figure 10. Service Orders Plan Data Overview

In OP2023 FP3 a service order can be relevant for billing in both options (root, non-root). Please see an example of the Financial View of Service Document from OP2023 FP3. Currently, only "Billing on completion" billing relevance is supported in OP2023 FP3 for the root item. 

Figure 11. Financial View of Service Hierarchy Setup with Root Item in OP2023 FP3

In this case, the plan data is updated, and plan revenues from service orders are included in the revenue recognition percentage-of-completion calculation.

Figure 12. Service - Plan/Actuals Report (OP2023 FP3 System)

 

4.2 Actual Postings

4.2.1 Actual Postings Difference (Root vs Non-Root Setup)

In the example below, a one-hour cost consumption posting takes place. The main difference is that for a service contract (SC) root item (blue box), revenue recognition postings (business transaction type = "TBRR") are executed at the service contract level rather than at the service order level, which is relevant for a non-root item (red box).

Figure 13. PM Order Cost Confirmations & Real-Time EBRR Postings

Revenue recognition posting amounts differ due to different revenue recognition methods assigned to service orders and contracts.

 

4.2.2 Cost Consumption Postings

In our example, we use the Cost-Based POC revenue recognition key for the root item hierarchy setup.

The planned costs are 2834,30

Figure 14. Total Plan Costs for Percentage Of Completion Calculation

The planned revenue is 3560,00:

Figure 15. Total Plan Revenues for Percentage Of Completion Calculation

  • Cost-based POC: 69,17/2834,30 = 2,44%
  • EBRR Posting: EUR 3560,00 * 2,44% = EUR 86,88

In document 2300000006, business transaction type “RMRU”, 1 hour of the agreed service is confirmed. Costs are posted from the cost center to the service order. Consequently, the account, order, and cost center are updated by EUR 69,17. In the document 100000026 business transaction type “TBRR”, the corresponding revenue is recognized based on the POC (in this case 2,44%). Hence, the Event-Based Revenue Recognition posting debits the Accrued Revenue account and credits the Revenue Adjustment account by EUR 86,88.

Figure 16. Activity Allocation & Real-Time EBRR Postings

4.2.3 Revenue Postings

Let’s post partial revenue posting (EUR 1200). The revenue is fully deferred.

Figure 17. Partial Revenue Posting & Real-Time EBRR Posting

Billing takes place in the document 9400000018 business transaction type “SD00”. The invoice refers to the first billing plan item. The whole revenue amount is deferred with the document 10000030 business transaction type “TBRR”.

 

4.2.4 EBRR Closing Run

After real-time confirmation and billing postings, we see deferred and accrued revenue balances in the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents Fiori App.

Figure 18. Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents: Overview

In the period-end closing, which can be done in the Run Revenue Recognition – Service Documents app, Event-Based Revenue Recognition revalues the balance sheet position of the selected service contract item. During this calculation, POC is limited to 100%. The actual POC is calculated based on the total costs incurred up to the calculation period and the actual plan costs. Furthermore, the balance of Accrued Revenue and Deferred Revenue is cleared up during period-end closing.

Figure 19. Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents Fiori App: Simulation

Figure 20. Period-End Event Based Revenue Recognition Posting

 

4.3 Technical Completion

After all confirmation and billing documents are posted, you can set the status to “completed” to the service order and execute the CRMS4_SRV_TECH_COMPL transaction to set the status to technically completed.

Figure 21. Set Technically Completed Status for Service Orders

The program can be executed manually. However, the general idea is to schedule the periodic program execution. 

Now, the service contract can also be set to technically complete.

Figure 22. Set Technically Completed Status for Service Contract

All EBRR adjustments are reversed during the EBRR closing run.

Figure 23. Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents Fiori App: Simulation

 

4.4 Manual Costs Planning Use Case

You can also consider external cost planning. Let’s look at an example:

You create a contract for a specific period. Each month, you bill your customer according to your billing plan (100 EUR). However, you cannot predict all maintenance events (planned costs) in advance. You also don’t know when maintenance will be needed for this specific contract.

Table 2. Use Cases - Actual Costs & Revenues Distribution Per Period

For example, in Contract 1, you have two maintenance events, requiring two service orders. The first repair was executed in April for 310 EUR, and the second repair took place in August for 700 EUR.

For Service Contract 2, there is only one repair in November for 300 EUR.

For Service Contract 3, there are two repairs totalling 1,900 EUR.

Since maintenance events occur unpredictably, it is impossible to create all service orders in advance and have planned costs updated automatically.

However, you do know the average margin per contract type. Let’s assume it is 20 percent. In this case, you can calculate planned costs externally and upload them using the "Financial Data Upload" Fiori app or SAC.

In EBRR customizing, you can select externally planned costs instead of automatically updated.

 

5 System Configuration

In the section below, I provide a full set of customizing activities to prepare the system for a hierarchical setup (root service contract item), including the introduction of a new service contract item category, new plan categories, and a complete EBRR setup.

If you have advanced configuration knowledge of hierarchical item-based services integration, please refer to 4.1.1 New Hierarchical Item Category Definition and 4.3.3 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Recognition Keys. These configuration steps are new.

 

5.1 Customizing – Service Contracts

5.1.1 New Root Service Contract Item Category Definition

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Define Item Categories -> Assignment of Business Transaction Categories -> BUS2000112 Service Contract

IMG Object

CRMV_ITEM_MA

Transaction Code

S_AEC_66000039

Table/View/ViewCluster

CRMC_SRV_CONT_I

A new item category must be defined for the hierarchical integration setup as a "root" item. The standard item category "SCN" can be used as a base for copying. The only difference from the "SCN" standard service contract item category is that the item must be marked as a "Root Item."

Figure 24. Service Contract Item Configuration - Root Item Flag

You can see the new hierarchical parameter “IS_HIER_PROC_ACTIVE” in the table CRMC_SRV_CONT_I.

 

5.1.2 Update Item Category Determination

The newly created item category must be assigned to the service contract type and item category group.

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Define Item Category Determination

IMG Object

CRMV_IT_ASSIGN

Transaction Code

S_AEC_66000040

Table/View/ViewCluster

CRMC_IT_ASSIGN

Figure 25. Item Category Determination Update

Afterwards, you should be able to choose your item category in the service contract.

Figure 26. Service Contract Creation – New Item Category is added to the Dropdown List

 

5.1.3 Assign Service Transaction Type to Billing Document Request Types

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Basic Functions -> Billing -> Assign Service Transaction Types to Billing Document Requests

IMG Object

CRMS4VC_BILL_PRTY_MAP

Transaction Code

S_AG9_66000013

Table/View/ViewCluster

CRMS4VC_BILL_PRTY_MAP (View)

Figure 27. Service Contract Item Configuration – Item Mapping to Sales Document Type

 

5.1.4 Assign Billing Plan to Item Category

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Assign Billing Plan Type to Item Category

IMG Object

CRMV_BILP_BT_IT

Transaction Code

S_A2C_40000042

Table/View/ViewCluster

CRMC_BILP_BT_IT

Figure 28. Service Contract Item Configuration – Mapping to Billing Plan Type

 

5.1.5      Copy Control Customizing

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Copying Control for Business Transactions -> Define Copying Control for Item Categories

IMG Object

CRMV_IT_COPY_MA

Transaction Code

S_A2C_40000015

Table/View/ViewCluster

CRMC_IT_COPY_MA

Figure 29. Service Contract Item Configuration - General Control Data

 

5.1.6 CRMS4C_PM_OT_MAP Table Clearance

When releasing a service order item, you may encounter the CRMS4_SRV_MAINT 060 error message. As a result, the maintenance order has not been created.

To resolve this issue, check the entries in the CRMS4C_PM_OT_MAP table. This table should be empty.

Figure 30. CRMS4C_PM_OT_MAP Table Check

You can check the 3433802 note (https://me.sap.com/notes/3433802/E) for additional details.  

 

 5.2 Customizing – Plan Categories (CO)

You may have already defined the necessary plan categories. For example, you can use service orders with advanced execution and percentage-of-completion revenue recognition methods. In this case, there is no need to duplicate your entries.

If the plan category has not been defined, please ensure it is maintained.

 

5.2.1 Check Plan Category for Maintenance Orders

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Planning -> Application-Specific Categories -> Maintain Categories for Maintenance Orders

IMG Object

FCOMV_CATEGORY_E

Transaction Code

S_E91_86000108

Table/View/ViewCluster

FCOMC_CATEGORY

Figure 31. Plan Categories for Maintenance Order

This plan category is used to update plan data for service orders with advanced execution in the Plan Data Line Items table (ACDOCP).

  • "PMORDER02" plan data is updated each time the service order is changed or saved.
  • "PMORDER03" plan data is updated at the moment of the service order release.

The planned cost data for the execution item is calculated in the execution order:

  • Planned activities = planned cost rate × planned working hours

Planned components = material net price × planned quantity

 

5.2.2 Check Plan Category for Service Orders

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Planning -> Maintain Categories for Service Documents

IMG Object

FCOMV_CATEGORY_F

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11001495

Table/View/ViewCluster

FCOMC_CATEGORY

Figure 32. Plan Categories for Service Order / Service Contract

In our case, the plan categories are used to update planned revenues for the service contract in the Plan Data Line Items table (ACDOCP).

  • "PSERV01" plan data is updated at the moment of the service order release.
  • "PSERV02" plan data is updated each time the service order is changed or saved.

 

5.2.3      Check / Maintain Virtual Category for Planning

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Planning -> Maintain Category for Planning

IMG Object

FCOMV_CATEGORY

Transaction Code

S_E91_86000108

Table/View/ViewCluster

FCOMC_CATEGORY

The application type of the item category should be “8 – Virtual Plan Category”. In this case you can sum up several plan categories together. In our case, it is mandatory.

Figure 33. Plan Category EBRR_SC_A Basic Configuration

 

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Planning -> Assign Plan Categories to a Virtual Category

IMG Object

FCOMV_CAT_ASSGMT

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11002399

Table/View/ViewCluster

FCOMC_CAT_ASSGMT

Figure 34. Plan Category EBRR_SC_A - Assignment to Source Plan Categories

 

5.2.4 Manual Cost Planning – Alternative Set Up (Optional)

Precise cost planning is essential, and having the correct revenue recognition adjustment postings is equally crucial. Some customers have long-term contracts and cannot accurately predict the exact amount of work required over the contract duration.

As a result, creating all service orders in advance is not feasible due to the uncertainty in volume and timing of maintenance. However, customers typically have an estimate of their average costs and margins per contract type. In such cases, it makes sense for them to plan costs externally.

External cost planning is possible with a standard “PLN Plan” plan category.

Figure 35. Plan Category PLN

You can also define a separate plan category.

Figure 36. Plan Category for External Costs Upload

The important parameter is that you allow the import of plan data. In addition, you must define a separate virtual plan category to be used later in EBRR customizing.

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> General Controlling -> Planning -> Assign Plan Categories to a Virtual Category

IMG Object

FCOMV_CAT_ASSGMT

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11002399

Table/View/ViewCluster

FCOMC_CAT_ASSGMT

Figure 37. Virtual Plan Category (Costs - External Upload, Revenues - Billing Plan)

 

5.3 Customizing – Event-Based Revenue Recognition

EBRR (Event-Based Revenue Recognition) postings are triggered by CO (COIN) of FI (RWIN) source documents only. Please find examples of such documents below:

  • COIN – CO Document
    • Activity Allocation (KB21n, IW41)
  • RWIN – FI Document
    • FI document (like FB01, FB60)
    • SD Billing (VF01)
    • Material Consumption (MIGO, 261 Movement Type)

 

5.3.1 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Sources

The first check/filter is the relevance of a document item GL account for EBRR post-processing.

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Sources

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_SOURCE

In our example, I simplify the setup because it is not specific to our particular use case. If you are looking for more advanced knowledge, please refer to the following resources:

  • SAP Note 2778479
  • SAP Note 3259335
  • Configuration of G/L Accounts in Event-Based Revenue Recognition

Figure 38. EBRR Source Configuration for Costs Selection

Figure 39. EBRR Source Configuration for Revenues Selections

 

5.3.2 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Assignment Rules

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Assignment Rules

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

SM34 (FINS_TRR)

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_ASS

You can reuse already existing rules or create a new one.

Figure 40. EBRR GL Posting Rules Configuration

You can see the relevant GL accounts with descriptions below:

Table 3. List of Relevant EBRR GL Accounts

 

5.3.3 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Recognition Keys

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Recognition Keys

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/View Cluster

FINS_TRR_VAL

Figure 41. EBRR Revenue Recognition Key Configuration - General Data

  • The key is activated for the necessary company codes and accounting principles.
  • The field Revenue Recognition method is “3 – Cost Based POC”.
  • Please use your customized assignment rule and plan category.
  • The source for plan values should be “500 :Cost and revenue planning of all objects from ACDOCP ”
  • The field “Billable Determination method” is always empty (not relevant for services).

Figure 42. EBRR Revenue Recognition Key Configuration - Company Code & Accounting Principle Specific Data

 

5.3.4 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Document Types

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Document Types

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_ACP

Figure 43. EBRR - Document Types Configuration

Fields Description:

  • Plan Category - Plan Category for history of plan used by EBRR.
    • Description: The values for this Plan Category display the history of plan values used by the closing or re-evaluation of Event-Based Revenue Recognition.
    • Use: The history is recorded with respect to the financial period and the cost elements for the adjustment postings of recognized COGS and revenue.
    • Dependencies: A Plan category of the application type 03, Revenue Recognition should be used here. 
  • IFRS 15-Indicator (Not Relevant for Current Use Case) – No impact on our use case, not supported for Service Documents. 
  • Currency Handling (Not Relevant for Current Use Case)  - The various possibilities of currency handling offer to choose the
    • principal currency, which is the main currency of the closing run of revenue recognition.
    • currency calculations, which allow to evaluate the total balance sheet amounts and to separate currency effects.
    • base currency of currency calculations, which is the starting point of the currency translations for the currency calculations.
    • document currency of closing, which is the document currency used for the financial documents created by the closing run of event-based revenue recognition.
    • valuation date, which is the suggested valuation date for all financial documents of event-based revenue recognition.
  • Loss handling (Not Relevant for Current Use Case) - This function determines the loss handling for POC-type recognition methods. If a plan loss is expected, the planned loss should be posted as a loss provision into the income statement immediately.

 

5.3.5 Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Evaluation Scope

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Evaluation Scope incl. Financial Statement

IMG Object

FINS_TRR

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11000209

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_EVA

For our use case, the scope is 2: Realtime evaluation and re-evaluation at closing.

Figure 44. EBRR - Evaluation Scope incl. Financial Statement Configuration

5.3.6 Derivation of Recognition Key for Service Documents

The revenue recognition key derivation is based on the table below. We need to add our new service contract "root" item category to the list. 

IMG Path

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Product Cost Controlling -> Cost Object Controlling -> Product Cost by Sales Order -> Period-End Closing -> Event-Based Revenue Recognition -> Derivation of Recognition Key for Service Documents

IMG Object

FINS_TRR_DRK_CRM

Transaction Code

S_ER9_11001107

Table/View/ViewCluster

FINS_TRR_DRK_CRM

Figure 45. Revenue Recognition Derivation Configuration

entry.title: Customer Engagement Initiative 2025 on Business Event Logging
entry.content:
As part of the new CEI cycle for 2025 we launched a CEI project for Business Event Logging. Registration is open until March 28, 2025.

Business Event Logging allows you to create event logs in SAP S/4HANA systems and on SAP BTP ABAP Environment, based on Business Events and on Change Documents. The logs can be used to evaluate changes to your business documents, and to analyze your business processes with the help of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

Register here until March 28, 2025: Insights into Business Processes with Business Event Logging 

We plan to roll-out information about the usage of Business Event Logging, and collect your feedback and ideas.

Find more information about Business Event Logging here:

  • One Tool to Track Them All 
  • Using Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
  • Integrating Business Event Logging with SAP Signavio
  • Transform your Business Processes with SAP Signavio and SAP Build 

Looking forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise: Dos and Don’ts
entry.content:
Teaser

Did you ever wonder how transporting key user custom fields works in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise and which apps you should use to avoid common mistakes? 
Do you want to know what to consider regarding key user custom fields when you upgrade your S/4HANA system? 
If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you. 

 

Disclaimer

Please note that this blog does exclusively address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise and does not address SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
In this blog we use S/4HANA as an abbreviation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise. In addition, for key user custom fields we use the abbreviation custom fields.

 

Table of Contents

This blog post covers the following topics: 

  • Dos and Don’ts in the context of software logistics
  • What to consider regarding custom fields in upgrade situations 
  • General Dos and Don’ts regarding custom fields 
  • Dos and Don’ts regarding ABAP-managed fields (abbreviation AMF) 

 

Dos and Don’ts in the context of software logistics

When working with key user custom fields in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you use "software collections" to transport your custom fields. These collections act like a safety box, wrapping the technical transport process. This is different in S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise. There, you transport the custom field artifacts on your own using a standard transport mechanism. With this, you have more freedom, but there are many things that could go wrong. It can easily happen that the managed and unmanaged objects of a custom field aren't kept in sync, by forgetting to transport the unmanaged objects, for example. 

Here is a list of Dos SAP recommends: 

  • Use Fiori apps for transport handling (also refer to note 2660797)
    • Register Extensions for Transport to
      • assign key user items to a package 
      • assign custom fields to a transport 
    • Configure Software Packages to  
      • configure automatic transport or task handling for packages 
      • assign transports to packages 
  • Register a so-called job user for generation processes (also refer to SAP Help Portal). You must ensure that: 
    • the job user is provided in maintenance view ATO_PK_JOB_USER (transaction SM30) 
    • the job user has sufficient authorization for publishing (e.g. SAP_ALL profile) 
    • the job user is valid (exists in the correct client and is within validity range) 
    • operations (save/publish/delete) are performed in the correct client 

And here is a list of Don’ts: 

  • Never develop custom fields in several different development systems – use one development system only 
    • Implemented checks are system-specific. This means that no data of other systems is considered 
    • Generated artifact names depend on custom field names, which means that a clash of object names may occur 
  • Never edit custom fields in several systems. Only edit them in one development system: 
    • Custom fields and its generated artifacts have a dedicated source system. This means that there is a risk of system inconsistency in case of custom field deletion if generated objects exist only locally 

What to consider regarding custom fields in upgrade situations

There is one more crucial difference regarding lifecycle stability for custom fields when working in S/4HANA compared to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the upgrade procedure contains a task where custom field artifacts are technically adapted on the fly, due to changes in the SAP standard.  
This is different from the upgrade procedure in S/4HANA. In S/4HANA, there is a custom field analysis process figuring out whether an adaptation is necessary. If this process identifies an adaptation need, you'll see the following messages in the upgrade log: 

Message message id 'CFD_REGENERATE_OP' message number '001' ('Regeneration of Custom fields necessary; refer to SAP Note 2640715') 

Please note that the following message could be shown, too: 

Message message id 'CFD_REGENERATE_OP' message number '016' ('Regeneration of datasource extension necessary; refer to SAP Note 2640715') 

This second message tells you that the issue is not an issue with a custom field but rather with a key user datasource extension object. You can find more details in note 2640715.  

Please note that there's a difference between upgrading the development system or a test or production system. 

In case you are upgrading the development system and an adaptation need has been identified, you should run the following report: CFD_REGENERATE_IN_ONPREMISE. This report implements the needed adaptations. When you use it, make sure to always be on the newest note level of this report. 

In case you are upgrading a system different from the development system, and an adaptation need has been identified, don't run the report. Instead, import the adaptations which have been made in the development system. 

In some scenarios, things can get a bit more complicated. For instance, you might want to run a test upgrade. To do this, you copy the development system, giving it a different system ID. Alternatively, you might need to move your development system to another system, resulting in a new system ID. In these cases, it's important to note that custom fields are only editable in their original source system. They are not editable in other systems. To enable their editability, you have to follow note 3484189.  
Only do this in the exceptional situations described above. Never maintain your custom fields in more than one system. If you maintain your custom fields in more than one system, they will break. 

 

General Dos and Don’ts regarding custom fields 

There may be situations where you created a custom field with a certain name (custom field ID) and accidentally deleted it. Now, you want to re-create the custom field with exactly the same name of the deleted one. 
This can be done. First, check the context you're in: 

  • If the transport with the field creation has not been released, you can delete it and recreate the field, even when choosing an incompatible field type. 
  • If the transport with the field creation has been released, you can delete it and recreate the field only as long as the field type you chose is compatible (see also note 3328541) 

It may happen that you want to expose your custom field in an OData service (to make the field visible in a UI that gets its data from this OData service, for example) and this OData service can't be enabled in the Custom Fields app. In this case, the respective OData service is not enabled for custom fields. To solve this, you can create a respective extensibility enabling request via the customer influence channel. 
Please note that using an OData redefinition approach to add custom fields to OData service entities doesn't work and is not supported (Note 1574568 constraint 34). 

 

Dos and Don’ts in the context of ABAP-managed fields 

These Dos and Don'ts are relevant for you if you use the feature ABAP-Managed Fields (described based on a concrete example in the blog post Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields). ABAP-managed fields allow you to convert your manually created append fields into real key user custom fields.
The challenges regarding software logistics come from the fact that for ABAP-managed fields, some of the custom fields' artefacts are generated and managed by the Custom Fields framework, and some are customer-managed (such as the CDS Code View, the CDS Text View, data elements, append, and so on). As these customer-managed artifacts are not safe-guarded and controlled by the Custom Fields framework, one could easily break the custom field by changing these manually created objects in an incompatible way, or accidentally deleting them.  

Are ABAP-managed fields lifecycle-stable, and do they follow the clean core principles?

The answer is yes. Let's have a closer look at the details.  
An ABAP-managed field comes with a set of unmanaged objects: 

  • Data element and domain (manually created or SAP standard data element) 
  • Manually created append with the field type using the data element above 
  • Depending on the chosen semantic type, also a manually created CDS code view and CDS text view  

If all of these unmanaged objects are built with ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development, they automatically apply to the clean core rules. The ABAP-managed field is 100% clean core compliant and fully lifecycle-stable. 

In general, within your unmanaged objects, it's possible that you use non-C1 released objects. With that, you can't fully follow the clean core path any longer. 

Let's dive a little deeper into the unmanaged objects to better understand how critical certain things are in terms of upgrade stability: 

Data element and domain

If the data element or domain you use to type your ABAP-managed field is not an SAP standard object which is released for Use in Cloud Development, then this data element or domain could be changed incompatibly or even deleted with any updated version of S/4HANA. However, in a lot of cases it’s unlikely that this will happen because, for example, the data element is prominently used within the SAP standard (which means that it's used for the typing of key fields.) 

Append field

In some cases, the Extension Include Structure to which you add the field using an ABAP dictionary append is released for Extensibility in Cloud Development. Then, you can add this append in ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. In case the Extension Include Structure is not released for developers but only for key users, you can't add the DDIC append in ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development but only in Standard ABAP. This no longer completely follows the clean core principle, but can still be 100% upgrade-stable, as it’s protected with the C0-contract release for key users. Using ABAP language version Standard, there are no more lifecycle stability checks in place to prevent you from leaving the upgrade-stable way. Consider the following aspects to be 100% upgrade-stable: 

  • Make sure that you use ZZ as prefix of the field name, or your own customer namespace to avoid collisions with future standard fields or your own custom fields 
  • Make sure that you’re using the field suffix as defined in the respective business context in transaction SCFD_REGISTRY

CDS Code View and CDS Text View

In case your CDS view references SAP standard objects which are not released for Use in Cloud Development (CDS views, data elements, database tables, …) these objects may be changed incompatibly or even deleted with an updated version of S/4HANA. In this case, 100% upgrade stability is no longer guaranteed. 

In the worst case, if one of the non-C1-released SAP standard objects you reference has been changed incompatibly or has been deleted, it can cause issues during a system upgrade. You’d have to rework your affected unmanaged objects to solve these issues. 

You can get more information regarding ABAP-managed fields following these links:  

  • General handling: SAP note 2981484 
  • Enable ABAP-managed fields for database tables with proxy views: SAP note 2463257 
  • Re-creating a custom field: SAP note 3327309 

Golden rules when working with ABAP-managed fields

  • Use the ATO apps for transport handling (also refer to note 2660797) 
  • Only use ABAP-managed fields in the exceptional situations described above 
  • Use a suitable prefix for the append field (ZZ or a real namespace) 
  • Use a suitable suffix for the append field depending on the business context 
  • Ensure that these objects are kept in the same software component as the append structure with the database field itself 
  • Ensure that the append field also exists in subsequent systems. Either the append field is imported before or transported together with the custom field 
  • Never delete append fields via SE11 before disabling the custom field via SCFD_EUI 
  • Never modify or delete the value help view used in the AMF field of type List 
  • Never delete the data element used in the AMF field 
  • Never continue with the publishing or deletion of further custom fields if a previous publishing or deletion failed 

Now, you are done! Congratulations! With this, you are now equipped to 

  • handle software logistics for custom fields 
  • avoid common pitfalls in this context. 

Please note that this blog has been elaborated by a team of experts, and I want to 
thank Yasmina van Rooij (YasminavanRooij), Daniel Wachs (DanielWachs), and Georg Wilhelm (georg_wilhelm) for their extremely helpful contributions.

 

Feedback, Comments, or Questions

You’re cordially invited to provide feedback, comments, or questions in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics. 
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/). 

Other related blog posts: 

  1. Convert Manually Created Append Fields to Real Key User Custom Fields 
  2. How to implement a custom field of type “Code List based on CDS view” with a developer extensibility custom entity 
    How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTful Programming Model using remote function modules    
  3. How to configure the value help as a context-dependent value help 
  4. How to configure intent-based navigation on a key user custom field of type “Code List based on CDS View”    
    and in this case, how to easily find the fitting semantic object and semantic object parameter: Custom field type “Code list based on CDS view”: Finding/defining the right value help view and the right Semantic Object and Semantic Object Parameter for Intent-Based Navigation 
  5. Custom Fields in S/4HANA: How to control field properties like mandatory, read-only, and visibility 
  6. Key User Custom Fields in SAP S/4HANA SAP GUI UIs: How to control field properties (mandatory,...) 
  7. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on SEGW OData Services: How to control field properties 
  8. Key User Custom Fields in Fiori UIs based on OData Services exposed with Service Binding 
  9. Custom fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility 
  10. SAP Screen Personas: How to position key user custom fields exactly where you want them to be

entry.title: What's new in FP03 of Transportation Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023
entry.content:
We are proud to announce that today we have released Feature Package FP03 for S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023. This brings many new innovations for Transportation Management.

In this article I will talk about the most important innovations in TM.

First, let us take a look at the

AI Enhancements.

As you may already know from the announcements made for FPS02, we have made significant investments in AI-based features. With this feature package, we are now enhancing AI-based goods receipt analysis.
AI-assisted Goods Receipt Analysis also supports Shipment Documents with multiple deliveries and nested hierarchy structures.

Extraction of data from the shipping document

This means that you can now scan the shipping documents that accompany truck deliveries, analyze the documents, compare them to data already in your system, and create consignment orders with that data. This new feature detects multiple deliveries as well as deep packaging hierarchies.
If there is a mismatch, it is automatically detected and you can respond with error handling to correct the erroneous data in your consignment document.

Innovations for Advanced Shipping and Receiving

In the area of Adv. S&R we have three new innovations. The most important of them is:

GTS-Integration in Decentral TM/EWM for Advanced Shipping and Receiving

In a system landscape where the Advanced Shipping and Receiving Process is performed in a decentralized TM/EWM environment, SAP Global Trade Services can now be integrated for the export scenario. In this process, the compliance check in GTS can be triggered based on the freight unit or freight order. This can be done manually or the process can be fully automated.

Architectural Overview of GTS-Integration in Decentral TM/EWM & Process Flow

In addition, after loading in EWM, the export declaration can be triggered in GTS. The creation of the customs invoice can be included in this process with a completely new message choreography.

 

The second topic in the context of Adv. S&R is the weight/volume update of outbound delivery order items for unpacked products. With this enhancement, it is now possible to update the weight and volume information of unpacked products, such as bulk products that are weighted in EWM, and send it to TM.

Last but not least in the context of Adv. S&R is the ability to synchronize predecessor documents for Rail Freight Orders. With this enhancement, it's possible to directly update predecessor documents such as the freight unit or inbound delivery with different quantities (more or less of a product quantity or packages).

 

Further Enhancements in the Freight Document Execution

Further enhancements are available for multimodal processes in the air and in the ocean,

  • Automatically Set CU to Loaded on a Road Freight Order. This process enhancement makes it possible to automatically set a container unit to loaded at the latest when the truck is checked out at the loading location. This reduces the number of manual steps required.
  • Unassign, assign a container unit of a pick-up freight orders & Improved display of handling execution status in the overview tab for freight bookings. To further simplify document handling in multi-modal scenarios, new actions have been introduced. These new actions make assigning and unassigning container units to pick-up freight orders more efficient. New actions for deleting containers in a freight order are also available.
  • There is also an enhancement available in the TORGenericRequest_In service. With this enhancement, the carrier can now update header changes as well as actual departure dates/times.


Planning & Network Enhancements

A solution that many of you may have been waiting for is a new, innovative, and straightforward way to determine the carrier in a freight unit, consignment order, or freight order. This innovation greatly simplifies the carrier determination process by allowing a simple set of rules to be defined to find the right carrier. To do this, a new carrier determination profile has been introduced where simple criteria such as source and destination locations can be used to select the carrier. This new profile can be integrated with Excel for easier maintenance and a simulation report is also available.

The new profile can be assigned to order types.

Carrier determination in a freight order

This new feature is processed, for example, when a freight unit is created or data is changed. Or when a user manually creates a freight order, for example with the function 'Create Capacity Document based on groups'.

The carrier can be inherited from the previous document, or it can be created from scratch if the previous documents do not contain a carrier. Along with this new feature, mass change capabilities for the carrier are also introduced in the Transportation Cockpit. I think this really a awesome new feature.

 

There are also several other enhancements in Planning & Network. I'll mention just a few of them here.
Consideration of split breaks: A nice enhancement to driver time constraints is a topic that is defined in the EU driver regulations. According to these rules, breaks can be further split.

Another helpful feature introduced in embedded scheduling is that it avoids short loading or driving times before calendar breaks like weekends or non-working days.

Avoidance of activities prior to non-working days


These enhancements allow for more compact scheduling results and improved resource and driver utilization.

 

With this feature package, we are also improving the manual load planning capabilities. Specifically for 3D load planning, a new toolbar configuration is introduced that allows you to define which manual load planning actions should be displayed. Manual planning is also further supported with additional usability features such as better item selection (e.g. select all of the same stack, ...) and better item positioning (e.g. align, swap, mirror, lift up&down, ...). This helps the planner to use 3D load planning more efficiently.

Customized Toolbar with new actions for manual planning in 3D load planning

 

Another important feature is that street-level routing on the map now takes into account the departure time of the document stage. This means that time is now used on the map for more accurate routing.

Not to forget the performance improvements for the VSR Optimizer. These performance improvements are significant and you can find more information in this blog.

 

There are also a number of enhancements in the area of

ERP document integration

Let us first talk about 3 smaller topics in this area:

There will be a report available to activate ERP documents (MM/SD/LE) for integration with TM. This report is very helpful when TM is used for the first time and existing documents need to be activated for TM integration. Instead of having to manually find and modify documents to make them relevant for TM integration, these documents can now be easily activated by a report.

Another useful feature is the switch for the integration of Purchase Scheduling Agreements (PSAs). Various reports can be used to stop the integration of PSAs, deactivate or reactivate documents and, for example, trigger or deactivate nJIT SPCs.

Very often it is necessary to check if the components of a component group are dangerous goods. Therefore, a DG check on component group level is required. To achieve this, a new BAdI is introduced to pass the component for a classic Dangerous Goods check (PS&S). If the check for any component is positive, the component group header is marked as dangerous goods.

Integration of nJIT S2C Outbound Deliveries in TM

As with the integration of nJIT for inbound deliveries in FP2, we now offer the integration of nJIT for outbound deliveries. As in the inbound case, the logistically relevant product is the component group material. This means that the freight unit in TM is created at the component group level. The Advanced Shipping Notification can be sent with additional information such as slot number and JIT call reference.

Integration of nJIT Outbound delivery to TM

 

With FP02, we have introduced functionality in Freight Unit Building and Planning based on any unit of measure. Charging and settlement now also support any UoM at the product level.
This means that scale bases, calculation bases, rate tables, and agreements can now work with dimensionless units such as meters or pieces.

 Usage of any Unit of Measurement in charge calculation

 

 

This was the latest and greatest of Transportation Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023 FP03. 

S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2025 is already in the works. You can be curious about what will be delivered later this year. You can always get a preview of what is planned in the Roadmap Explorer.

Further information can be found here:

  • What’s New Viewer
  • SAP TM Product Page on sap.com
  • SAP Community for Transportation Management
  • SAP Transportation Management Group on LinkedIn

entry.title: How to: Extension of WebDynpro Configurations due to changes in CDS Views
entry.content:
The backend and WebDynPro must be properly configured to enable a field for filtering in a WebDynpro application. This involves modifying the CDS (Core Data Services) views to ensure the field is recognised and available for filtering in the frontend configuration.

This topic concerns these Fiori Apps, but the principle is the same for each WebDynpro App.

CDS Query (object)CDS Query (2C+SQL View)CDS Query WebDynpro ConfigurationApp ID
C_CostCenterQ2001CFICOSTCTRQ2001FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTCCF0963
C_CostCenterPlanActQ2001CFICCPLANACT2001FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTCC01W0081
C_ProfitCenterQ2701CFIPROFCTRQ2701FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPCF0959
C_ProfitCenterPlanActQ2701CFIPCPLANACT2701FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPC01W0079
C_MarketSegmentQ2501CFIMARKSEGMQ2501FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTMSF0960
C_MarketSegmentPlanActQ2501CFIMSPLANACT2501FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTMS01W0078
C_ProfitAndLossQ2901CFIPROFLOSSQ2901FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPLF0958
C_ProfitAndLossPlanActQ2903CFIPLPLANACTFIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPL03F1710
C_FunctionalAreasQ2801CFIFAQ2801FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTFAF1583A
C_FunctionalAreaPlanActQ2801CFIFAPLANACT2801FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTFA01W0077
C_InternalOrderPlanActQ2101CFIIOPLANACT2101FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTIO01W0076
C_SalesOrderQ2301CFISALESORDQ2301FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTSOF1582A
C_ProjectQ2201CFIPROJECTQ2201FIS_FPM_OVP_IPACTPR F0961
C_ProjectPlanActQ2201CFIPRPLANACT2201FIS_FPM_OVP_IPPLANACTPR01W0080

Backend changes in CDS views:

Variant 1: Extending the CDS View

If the field is not yet available for filtering, you must modify the CDS view and add the appropriate annotations.

  •  Create a new CDS view extension and ensure you use your naming conventions where required.

 Important: Replace placeholders such as ZXXCOSTEXT,  C_CostCenterQ2001, ZXX_C_COST_CENTER_EXT, and I_JournalEntryItemCube.SourceLedger with names relevant to your implementation. These correspond to:

  • Catalog name
  • CDS view name
  • CDS extension view name
  • Field name

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZXXCOSTEXT'
extend view C_CostCenterQ2001 with ZXX_C_COST_CENTER_EXT
{
@Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: true, mandatory: false }
I_JournalEntryItemCube.SourceLedger

}

 

  • Save and activate the CDS view

This will add Source Ledger to the C_CostCenterQ2001 query as a Filter. However, to see it within the Fiori App, you need to change the configuration.

Variant 2: Using Metadata Extension

If the field already exists in a standard CDS view but does not have a filter annotation, you can use a metadata extension instead of modifying the view directly:

  • Ensure Metadata Extensions Are Allowed (If not, you will need to use Variant 1.).
  • If you do not have this annotation present in the queries mentioned above, you will need to install this SAP Note 3509451 to your system.
  • Create a Metadata Extension

Important: Replace placeholders such as CostCenterQ2001 and CalendarWeek with names relevant to your implementation. These correspond to:

  • CDS view name
  • Field name

 

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
annotate entity C_CostCenterQ2001
    with 
{
    @Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: true, mandatory: false }
    CalendarWeek;
    
}

 

I would suggest you use @Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER, so it is determined what was done.

WebDynpro configuration:

  • Open the Fiori application Cost Centers – Actuals. Right-click in the filter area and select ‘Technical Help’.  Technical Help window will open.

  • In the technical help window, click on the shown area (Application Configuration).  A new window will open.

  • In the application configuration window, click on the Configuration Name.  You will be redirected to the Page Schema.

  • Scroll down to locate the Overview Page Schema. Expand the Page Header Area and then click on Config ID.

  • In the newly opened window, drag the desired filter into the Page Header Area within the schema below.

  • Click save.

Once all these steps are done you will see the field in the search filter among other fields like Ledger, Fiscal Year of Ledger etc., the position on where the field appears is up to you.

 

I would like to say a big thanks to my colleague, who helped me with creating this blog and preparing the documentation.

entry.title: The Case for Upgrades in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - New whitepaper published
entry.content:
Is your business still running an older version of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition? You could be missing out on critical innovations and putting your system at risk. Friendly reminder: Mainstream and extended maintenance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems on versions 1709, 1809, 1909, and 2020 will conclude in December 2025. Now is the perfect time to plan your upgrade and stay ahead with the latest features and support!

 

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

 

 Why Upgrade? It’s More Than Just a Task

Upgrading your SAP system isn't just about keeping up; it's about gaining a strategic advantage. Think of it as future-proofing your business. Here's what you stand to gain:

  • Supercharged Security: Outdated systems are vulnerable. Regular upgrades keep you protected with the latest cybersecurity measures and data protection. SAP only guarantees security updates for the most recent feature and support packages, so staying current is essential.
  • Innovation at Your Fingertips: Running on the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition gives you access to continuous innovation, including advanced business AI, automation, and predictive analytics. New features like the AI copilot Joule, Microsoft Teams integration, and SAP Fiori improvements boost productivity and user experience. (see full list here). These innovations are included in your subscription—make sure you don’t miss out!
  • Cut Costs and Complexity: Sticking with older versions often leads to costly and complex custom code. Regular upgrades in combination with a clean core approach can help you stay closer to the SAP standard, making your system more agile and easier to manage.
  • Maximize Your ROI: You've already invested in SAP. By upgrading, you're maximizing that investment. You're unlocking the full potential of your system and getting the most value out of your license.
  • Practice makes Perfect: It's like training a muscle. The more often you upgrade, the easier the process becomes. Regular upgrades equip your team with the know-how for faster, smoother projects with fewer disruptions. Customers like Hitachi High-Tech have reduced their upgrade projects to just a single month by adopting a regular upgrade approach. Read Hitachi´s complete upgrade story.

Build your Upgrade Routine:

Establishing your governance strategy and plan to fit your individual operation and needs is key. SAP recommends that you run on the latest release or at least one below the latest, aligning your IT change management and business release strategy, as well as your project schedule, with SAP S/4HANA release timelines. As announced with the new SAP S/4HANA release and maintenance strategy, new releases are delivered every second year, with three feature and support packages in between, offering you greater flexibility and easier-to-consume innovation.

SAP is Here to Help You Every Step of the Way

Upgrading doesn't have to be daunting. SAP provides a wealth of resources to ensure a smooth transition:

  • SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade, a methodology for implementing SAP solutions, guides your team through the upgrade by prompting deliverables and tasks at the right time.  
  • SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades helps assess your upgrade efforts with analysis of your custom code, compatibility scope usage, and add-on usage.  
  • SAP guidance and resources for technical upgrade execution are included with software subscription., including tasks like operating system upgrade, database upgrade, and the technical installation of SAP S/4HANA upgrades. Please remember that as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customer, you're responsible for planning upgrades, requesting services, and handling post-upgrade tasks.
  • The Enterprise Support Academy and dedicated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Value Maps* help upskill your team.  
  • SAP Services and Support including SAP Enterprise Support available with all cloud offerings, and several premium plans, can bring you a dedicated expert assistant for the short or long term.
  • SAP Partner Finder can help you identify a partner from our vast ecosystem for implementation services.  

 

In conclusion: By embracing regular upgrades and a clean core approach, you unlock a multitude of benefits, including accelerated innovation adoption and increased security and compliance. Building your upgrade routine and leveraging valuable tools, services and support from SAP and its ecosystem will result in a smoother upgrade journey.

So don’t let your system reach a point where a costly and time-consuming overhaul becomes essential to avoid slowdown. Maximize the value of your SAP investment - embrace agility and upgrade to stay ahead of the competition.

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

Join our Upgrade dedicated SAP Community

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • BTP AI based Data Anonymization Service
  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Multi-Region Desaster Recovery
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning, Integration with Joule 
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Concur

  • SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports
  • SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards
  • SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration
  • SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience
  • SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts
